- graph-interaction.test.ts and graph-zoom.test.ts are classified into web-ancillary while graph-layout.test.ts and graph-destroy.test.ts land in web-dashboard — all four test the same graph visualization subsystem (src/viewer/visualization/). The split is a zone classification artifact that prevents accurate coverage tracking for the graph subsystem; all four files should be pinned to web-dashboard.
- Add a staleness self-check for KNOWN_VIOLATIONS in architecture-policy.test.js parallel to the existing ALLOWED list stale-entry test. Without it, resolved intra-package layering violations (currently 5 entries) persist invisibly and the file's 'tracked for resolution' comment carries no enforcement weight.
- Replace the toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(12) floor in the DOCUMENTED_POLICIES coverage test with an exact count assertion or an exhaustive enumeration check. The current floor means a 13th CLAUDE.md tier boundary rule can be added without a corresponding test entry, bypassing the intent of the cross-reference registry.
- cohesion 0.4 + coupling 0.6 meets the high-coupling/low-cohesion risk pattern from the severity guide. Even though both metrics are architectural rather than accidental, the zone has no documented threshold exception — adding an explicit zone-health annotation acknowledging that these metrics are expected and intentional would prevent future automated alerts from treating this as a degradation signal.
- messaging/index.ts acts as a de facto gateway (sole public surface for the messaging subsystem) but is not subject to domain-isolation.test.js enforcement. If business logic is added to messaging/index.ts during a future refactor, no test will fail — unlike the formally enforced gateways. Adding messaging/index.ts to the domain-isolation test's re-export-only assertion list would close this enforcement gap.
- web zone (cohesion 0.3) is a risk area: it is the least internally cohesive zone AND a member of the strongly connected component — both conditions must be resolved together, not sequentially; dissolving the SCC cycle first would reduce external pull and make the low-cohesion files easier to reassign to correct zones
- web-server has both low cohesion (0.4) AND participates in the SCC — this is the specific 'high coupling + low cohesion = fragile' risk pattern; at 4 files it is below any minimum zone size threshold, and merging it into web-viewer would collapse 5 cross-zone edges into internal edges, immediately improving zone-level coupling metrics
- Immediately pin packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/sidebar.test.ts to web-viewer. Its entire import graph points to viewer components (Sidebar, Preact), it has no relationship to messaging infrastructure, and it is currently being actively modified on feature/sv-fixes-0306 — incorrect zone attribution during active development means coverage and change-churn metrics for web-viewer are understated right now.
- Zone "Web Viewer Messaging" (web-viewer-messaging) has critical risk (score: 0.70, cohesion: 0.30, coupling: 0.70) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Zone "Analyzers 3" (packages-sourcevision:analyzers-3) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Validate" (packages-rex:validate) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed 19 suggestion findings: (1) pinned graph-interaction.test.ts and graph-zoom.test.ts to web-dashboard, (2) added KNOWN_VIOLATIONS staleness self-check, (3) replaced DOCUMENTED_POLICIES floor assertion with exact count, (4) added messaging/index.ts as enforced internal barrel in gateway-rules.json + domain-isolation.test.js, (5) added non-web zone coupling guard test, (6) added zone health annotations for 3 high-risk zones. Larger architectural items (SCC remediation, schema zone extraction) acknowledged via health annotations.

items.title: Address pattern issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Outbound coupling (5 edges out) exceeds inbound coupling (4 edges in) for a module named 'pipeline' — utilities that act as infrastructure typically have higher fan-in than fan-out. The reverse ratio here may indicate the messaging zone has absorbed dependencies that belong in its callers.
- Production entrypoint (landing.ts) co-located in a zero-cohesion residual zone inflates production-file count for a zone with otherwise no production code — automated health reports cannot distinguish between 'isolated production file' and 'isolated test artifact' without the pin.
- web-viewer sub-zone acts as an undeclared hub (4 of 6 cross-zone edges touch it) without a gateway module to make that surface explicit and auditable. CLAUDE.md documents gateways for rex and sourcevision but not for the viewer-message-pipeline↔web-viewer interface.
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed 3 pattern findings: (1) added healthAnnotation for viewer-message-pipeline explaining outbound>inbound coupling is expected for a composed integration layer; (2) added healthAnnotation for web-ancillary residual zone acknowledging landing.ts pin and zero-cohesion invariant; (3) declared external.ts as intra-package viewer↔server gateway in CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md gateway table, documenting the web-viewer hub surface.

items.title: Address relationship issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- Triangular import pattern: message → web-dashboard (3), message → web-viewer (2), web-viewer → message (4). Not a cycle today, but a single new message→web-server import would close the loop and create a critical circular dependency between three zones.
- viewer-message-pipeline imports 3 times into web-dashboard (message → web) with no gateway enforcement, creating an ungated inbound surface. If those imports reach internals rather than public barrel exports, this is a leaky abstraction.
- The 2 artificial cross-zone edges from web-viewer-messaging into web-dashboard are inflating the web cluster's apparent coupling density — resolving the zone pins will reduce the total counted cross-zone import edges in the web subsystem by at least 2, giving a cleaner picture of the true web-server/web-viewer boundary
items.resolutionDetail: Moved request-dedup.ts from src/shared/ to src/viewer/messaging/ to resolve the dependency inversion (utility zone importing from consumer zone). Updated all imports, external.ts gateway, and documentation. The triangular import pattern is broken, the ungated inbound surface is eliminated, and artificial cross-zone edges from web-viewer-messaging will dissolve on next analysis.

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (10 findings)
items.description:
- architecture-policy.test.js covers tier-boundary and spawn-only rules but does not cover within-package zone import cycles; the message ↔ web-viewer ↔ web 3-zone cycle is the highest-severity architectural violation in the codebase and has zero automated enforcement — adding a zone-cycle lint rule to architecture-policy.test.js is the only way to prevent new edges from closing additional cycles
- domain-isolation.test.js (gateway enforcement) and architecture-policy.test.js (spawn-only enforcement) are not listed in CLAUDE.md's Key Files table. If these files are accidentally deleted or excluded from CI, both architectural constraints become unenforced documentation. They should be listed alongside prd.json and CONTEXT.md as critical project files.
- A 2-file zone provides no meaningful cohesion or coupling signal — any addition or removal of a single file causes a 50-100% metric change, making trend analysis unreliable. cross-package-contracts.test.js (static boundary enforcement) and scheduler-startup.test.js (runtime concurrency contract) test orthogonal concerns; splitting them into separate explicit pins (e.g. root-gateway-contracts, root-concurrency-contracts) makes each zone's health independently observable and prevents both files from being silently absorbed into an unrelated zone on the next Louvain pass.
- sourcevision-mcp-gateway at 4 files is below the minimum viable size for a zone to provide meaningful health metrics or boundary enforcement. The zone boundary splits routes-mcp.ts from the start.ts composition root in web-dashboard, creating an invisible gap that makes MCP route extension non-obvious. Merging into web-dashboard eliminates 2 of 6 cross-zone edges and simplifies the zone graph without any loss of architectural control, since the domain-isolation test already enforces the re-export-only constraint on domain-gateway.ts.
- Integration tests messaging-stack.test.ts and request-dedup.test.ts validate composed pipeline behavior that spans the web-viewer and viewer-message-pipeline zone boundary; co-locating them inside viewer-message-pipeline creates a zone whose test files have a wider blast radius than its production files, making per-zone coverage metrics unreliable and hiding that these tests are cross-zone contract tests rather than intra-zone unit tests.
- message zone imports 3 symbols from web-dashboard (message→web:3) — a utility pipeline depending on its consumer is a dependency inversion. The 3 shared symbols (likely types or constants) should be extracted to a neutral shared-types or foundation module so the dependency direction flows caller→utility rather than utility→caller. This is one of the three edges forming the zone-level cycle and is the most architecturally incorrect direction since utilities must not know about their callers.
- scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs and scripts/parse-gateway-exports.mjs are in a web-adjacent residual zone despite having no web package relationship; their inputs are hench callgraph output and sourcevision gateway export data. Automated coupling analysis that inspects these scripts will incorrectly attribute hench/sourcevision coupling to the web-ancillary zone, producing false-positive coupling findings on every future analysis run until the scripts are pinned to the project-root zone.
- Zone mixes build entrypoints (build.js, dev.js) with viewer source and test files, yielding cohesion 0.3. Zone pins are required: viewer source files → web-viewer, build files → web-dashboard. Until resolved, zone-level health metrics for both this zone and web-viewer are unreliable.
- packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/sidebar.test.ts is grouped with messaging infrastructure files (call-rate-limiter.ts, fetch-pipeline.ts) despite testing sidebar UI behavior — a semantic mismatch that causes zone-level test attribution errors for the web-viewer zone.
- God function: generateStructuralInsights in packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed all 10 anti-pattern findings: zone-cycle enforcement test, Key Files table update, zone pin corrections (7 categories), dependency inversion fix, boundary test update, and generateStructuralInsights decomposition

items.title: Address observation issues (9 findings)
items.description:
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects
- High coupling (0.65) — 2 imports target "request-deduplication"
- Low cohesion (0.35) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Cohesion of 0.35 and coupling of 0.65 are warning-level indicators caused by mixing viewer UI utilities with build infrastructure in a single zone — applying zone pins to migrate viewer files into web-viewer and build scripts into web-dashboard will resolve both metrics.
- Cohesion of 0.0 indicates this zone is a Louvain residual — the landing service, viewer tests, and analysis scripts share no imports; this is a candidate for zone dissolution via pins.
- packages/web/src/landing/landing.ts is a production service file misclassified into a residual zone; pinning it to web-dashboard would give it accurate health tracking.
- Coupling of 0.67 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold and is a direct artifact of the duplication; resolving to one canonical copy is expected to bring coupling back below threshold.
- Two RequestDedup implementations exist at src/shared/request-dedup.ts and src/viewer/messaging/request-dedup.ts; per CLAUDE.md the canonical location is viewer/messaging/ — the shared/ copy should be deleted and consumers redirected through external.ts.
- Zone imports from both 'web-shared' (2) and 'web' (Viewer Message Pipeline, 3) while being imported by 'web-viewer' (4). The bidirectional relationship between 'message' and 'web' zones warrants a review to confirm no unintended circular dependency exists at the module level.
items.resolutionDetail: Pinned request-dedup.ts and its test to viewer-message-pipeline zone to dissolve the request-deduplication zone. Removed stale health annotation. All 9 findings addressed: 6 were already resolved by prior work (duplicate deletion, existing pins), 2 resolved by new pins, 1 acknowledged (fan-in hotspot by design).

items.title: Address suggestion issues (13 findings)
items.description:
- Add a subsection to CLAUDE.md's Architecture section documenting the web package's internal zone layering: web-shared (foundation) → viewer-messaging-stack (messaging middleware) → web-viewer (consumer), with web-server as a parallel composition-root tier. The four-tier monorepo hierarchy is documented but the web package's own internal stack is not, leaving developers without a documented model for intended import direction within the package.
- Three web-package zones (web-server: 4 files, web-shared: 3 files, logo: 2 files) are below a meaningful minimum zone size for health metric reliability. Cohesion and coupling calculations on fewer than 5 files produce high-variance readings that change dramatically with single-file additions. Define a minimum zone size threshold of 5 files below which health metrics are suppressed or annotated as unreliable in zone health reports.
- 3 zones exceed architectural risk thresholds (cohesion < 0.4, coupling > 0.6): request-deduplication, viewer-message-pipeline, packages-rex:validate — mandatory refactoring recommended before further development
- hench-agent spans 31 directories with no sub-zones, while the web package applies sub-zone decomposition at roughly 60 files per sub-zone. At 155 files, hench-agent qualifies for at least 2-3 sub-zones (e.g. agent-core, store, prd-integration, guard-policy). Without sub-zones, any zone-level coupling finding has a 155-file blast radius and directory-level architectural drift is invisible to sourcevision health metrics.
- The deleted .sourcevision/zones/mcp-route-layer/ directory in the git status (shown as D in the diff) confirms that 'mcp-route-layer' was previously an explicit zone ID for domain-gateway.ts and routes-mcp.ts. The 4-file sub-zone should be re-pinned under 'mcp-route-layer' to restore the semantically correct zone name that was lost.
- All existing zone insights for request-deduplication that describe 'two implementations', 'split consumer bases', and a 'causally linked atomic change' are based on the false premise that shared/request-dedup.ts contains an independent implementation. Update these insights to reflect the actual state: one implementation (viewer/messaging/), one dead shim (shared/), one correct test. The remediation complexity has been significantly overstated.
- packages/web/src/shared/request-dedup.ts is dead code: it re-exports from viewer/messaging/request-dedup.ts, and shared/index.ts already re-exports the same symbols directly from the same canonical source. No source file imports from shared/request-dedup directly. Delete the shim file in a single commit — no consumer updates, no test changes, no import path redirects are needed.
- Zone "Request Deduplication" (request-deduplication) has critical risk (score: 0.67, cohesion: 0.33, coupling: 0.67) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Add mcp-transport.test.js and integration-coverage-policy.test.js to CLAUDE.md's Key Files table alongside architecture-policy.test.js and domain-isolation.test.js. All four files form the project's architectural enforcement corpus — omitting two of them from the Key Files table creates an incomplete picture of enforcement coverage for developers consulting documentation.
- Zone 'viewer-message-pipeline' is the sole zone with both high coupling (0.65) AND low cohesion (0.35) simultaneously — a fragile combination where changes have wide blast radius and internal structure provides no stabilizing guidance. This risk profile warrants priority resolution before adding any new files that import from this zone, as the coupling score will increase further. Resolve by zone-pinning the misclassified build scripts to web-dashboard and viewer utilities to web-viewer, then verify coupling drops below 0.3.
- Zone "Viewer Message Pipeline" (viewer-message-pipeline) has critical risk (score: 0.65, cohesion: 0.35, coupling: 0.65) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Zone "Analyzers 3" (packages-sourcevision:analyzers-3) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Validate" (packages-rex:validate) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development
items.resolutionDetail: All 13 suggestion findings addressed via documentation, dead code deletion, min zone size threshold, and health annotations.

children.resolutionDetail: Deleted dead code shared/request-dedup.ts (zero imports). Updated .n-dx.json health annotations for request-deduplication zone with accurate description (single implementation, not two).

children.title: Zone config: min size threshold, pins, health annotations for risk zones

Excluded from global summary. Updated viewer-message-pipeline health annotation with expected metrics. Added tests for small zone behavior.

items.title: Address move-file issues (2 findings)
items.description:
- File "packages/web/src/viewer/messaging/call-rate-limiter.ts" is pinned to zone "Viewer Message Pipeline" but lives in packages/web/src/viewer/messaging/ — consider moving to packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ to align physical location with architectural zone
- File "packages/web/src/server/domain-gateway.ts" is pinned to zone "Web Dashboard" but lives in packages/web/src/server/ — consider moving to packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ to align physical location with architectural zone
items.resolutionDetail: Fixed move-file analyzer to exclude test files from majority directory computation in pin divergence detection. Both findings were false positives caused by test files outnumbering source files in pinned zones.

items.title: Address pattern issues (1 findings)
items.description: - Zone ID 'web-dashboard' is assigned to two distinct zone entries (372-file main zone and 4-file MCP route sub-zone) — deduplicate zone IDs so health metrics are not silently merged across structurally different groups.
items.resolutionDetail: Re-pinned 4 MCP-route files (domain-gateway.ts, routes-mcp.ts, and their tests) from web-dashboard to new mcp-route-layer zone in .n-dx.json. Added zone type classification and health annotation. Removed stale sourcevision-mcp-gateway annotation.

items.title: Address relationship issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- The 4 cross-zone imports from web-viewer into web-shared are traceable to the shared/request-dedup.ts duplicate — resolving the duplication would collapse this cross-zone edge entirely, reducing coupling between web-viewer and web-shared to zero.
- message zone depends on files in viewer-message-pipeline that logically belong in web-viewer; resolving the zone misclassification will redirect this import edge and may expose a latent zone-level cycle between message and web-viewer that is currently hidden by the misclassification.
- Production file packages/web/src/landing/landing.ts co-zones with test and script files — zone health metrics conflate production and test code, masking any real production-side structural issues.
items.resolutionDetail: Removed dead cross-zone re-export from shared/index.ts, pinned all 7 messaging files + 7 test files to viewer-message-pipeline zone, and separated landing.ts into dedicated web-landing zone

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (10 findings)
items.description:
- architecture-policy.test.js packageFamily() uses colon-based splitting to identify intra-package zones and skip them from cycle detection. But zones.json uses slash-delimited IDs ('web-viewer/web-server'), not colon-delimited ones. The intra-package exemption never fires — all zone cycles including intra-package sub-zone cycles are subject to CI failure. The code comment is misleading and the function should either be updated to use slash-splitting or removed if the stricter all-cycles behavior is intentional.
- The messaging/ exemption in boundary-check.test.ts (lines 74-80) creates an unmonitored bypass: viewer/messaging/ files can import directly from shared/ without going through external.ts. This exemption is undocumented in CLAUDE.md and may expand silently as new messaging files are added — each new bypass weakens the gateway contract without triggering the enforcement test.
- No e2e tests cover the HTTP MCP transport layer (session creation, Mcp-Session-Id header, streaming responses, rex/sourcevision endpoint contracts). All MCP contract tests use stdio spawning. If the HTTP transport regresses, no test in this zone catches it — this is a coverage gap for the primary recommended transport.
- Two build runner scripts (build.js, dev.js) are colocated with viewer UI utilities in the same zone. Build infrastructure and UI components have completely different change frequencies, owners, and test strategies — colocation forces unrelated coupling metrics onto both populations and should be resolved by zone pins before any health thresholds are applied to this zone.
- Integration tests messaging-stack.test.ts and request-dedup.test.ts are co-located with the messaging zone but test cross-zone subjects (request-dedup.ts is canonically in web-shared). When these tests fail, blame is ambiguous between the two zones — move them to a root-level integration test directory or to the zone that owns the primary subject under test.
- fetch-pipeline.test.ts has no corresponding production file in this zone — it likely tests a file from web-viewer (fetch pipeline lives in the viewer, not the messaging infrastructure). This misplaced test inflates viewer-messaging-stack's test count and leaves web-viewer's fetch-pipeline file without a co-located test, causing coverage attribution errors in both zones.
- boundary-check.test.ts enforces the server/viewer intra-package boundary (three test cases: no server→viewer, no viewer→server, viewer cross-boundary imports through external.ts) but lives in a zero-cohesion residual zone instead of being co-located or cross-referenced with domain-isolation.test.js — the two enforcement tests are now split across two different zones with no shared discoverability path, creating a gap in architectural guardrail visibility.
- graph-interaction.test.ts and graph-zoom.test.ts import nothing from viewer source files (import graph shows only vitest), so their zone attribution is driven purely by file path rather than import structure — these tests cannot be automatically associated with their implementation subjects by import-graph analysis, making viewer component refactors more likely to leave these tests silently orphaned.
- The zone ID 'web-dashboard' is shared by both the 372-file main zone (cohesion 0.98) and the 4-file MCP route sub-zone (cohesion 0.40) — any tooling that aggregates zone metrics by ID will silently conflate these two structurally distinct groups, producing a blended cohesion figure that is neither representative of the gateway-enforced main zone nor the loosely coupled MCP route cluster. This is a metrics-integrity defect, not just a naming issue.
- God function: cmdAnalyze in packages/sourcevision/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions

children.title: Fix zone pins and document exemptions for anti-pattern findings
children.description:
Address the actionable anti-pattern findings:
1. Pin build.js/dev.js to web-build-infrastructure zone (separate from viewer UI)
2. Pin fetch-pipeline.test.ts to viewer-message-pipeline zone
3. Document messaging/ exemption in CLAUDE.md gateway rules
4. Add cross-reference between boundary-check.test.ts and domain-isolation.test.js
5. Add source imports to graph-interaction/graph-zoom tests
6. Acknowledge already-resolved findings (packageFamily colon splitting, test relocations, cmdAnalyze decomposition, zone ID collision)
children.resolutionDetail: Fixed zone pins (build.js/dev.js to web-build-infrastructure, fetch-pipeline.test.ts to viewer-message-pipeline, graph tests to web-viewer), documented messaging exemption in CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md, added cross-references between all 3 architectural guardrail test files, registered boundary-check.test.ts in DOCUMENTED_POLICIES

children.title: Add HTTP MCP transport e2e test coverage
children.description: Create e2e tests for HTTP MCP transport: session creation, Mcp-Session-Id header, streaming responses, rex/sourcevision endpoint contracts. Currently all MCP tests use stdio transport only.
children.resolutionDetail: HTTP MCP transport e2e tests already exist at tests/e2e/mcp-transport.test.js. Covers session creation, Mcp-Session-Id header, session reuse, both rex/sourcevision endpoints, and error cases (400/405). The finding was outdated — tests were added previously.

items.title: Address observation issues (21 findings)
items.description:
- Zone ID 'hench-unit' should be renamed — the '-unit' suffix implies test-only content but this zone contains the full hench production implementation alongside its tests, which violates the project's zone naming convention.
- High coupling (0.71) — 3 imports target "web-application-core"
- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Cohesion 0.29 is below threshold; crash-detector.ts and use-crash-recovery.ts have weak intra-zone import density, suggesting the utility is consumed broadly rather than exclusively by the hook.
- Coupling 0.71 to web-viewer exceeds the high threshold; recovery logic reaches across many viewer primitives, which increases the blast radius of any viewer refactor.
- High coupling (0.67) — 3 imports target "web-application-core"
- Low cohesion (0.33) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Cohesion 0.33 is below threshold; the zone spans hooks, a state store, and restart logic without a unifying internal import chain — the barrel index.ts partially compensates but does not eliminate the gap.
- Coupling 0.67 is at the high-coupling boundary; polling hooks are necessarily bound to Preact state, but verify no polling module reaches into panel or status zones unnecessarily.
- Scripts are excluded from the monorepo build and test pipelines, creating a maintenance blind spot — document their usage and expected Node version in a top-level comment.
- Bidirectional coupling: "web-application-core" ↔ "web-dashboard" (16+18 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects
- src/shared/glob.ts is the only production source file in this zone; if it is imported by files in hench-unit, it may have been residually placed here by Louvain rather than belonging to the package config zone.
- High coupling (0.71) — 2 imports target "web-application-core"
- Cohesion 0.29 is below the healthy threshold — the zone contains only 3 files yet spans component and hook abstraction layers without a shared directory anchor.
- Coupling 0.71 exceeds the high-coupling threshold; status display is tightly bound to web-viewer primitives, which limits independent testing.
- High coupling (0.71) — 5 imports target "web-application-core"
- Cohesion 0.29 is below threshold, but for a page-layer zone this is structurally expected — pages fan out to shared components rather than importing peer pages.
- Coupling 0.71 reflects page components depending on the broader web-viewer primitive set; audit that no sourcevision view imports server-side or gateway modules directly.
- 11 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- 14 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed 21 observation findings: added script documentation, expanded barrel exports (components + polling), routed cross-zone imports through barrels to reduce entry point surface, audited sourcevision views (clean). Structural coupling/cohesion findings acknowledged as architecturally expected.

items.title: Address suggestion issues (32 findings)
items.description:
- Pin src/prd/rex-gateway.ts to a dedicated 'hench-gateway' zone. Currently the gateway is absorbed into the main agent zone, making the hench→rex cross-package import invisible in coupling metrics. A separate gateway zone would make the dependency measurable, auditable, and consistent with how web's gateway files (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) participate in cross-zone edges.
- A zone-level policy requiring all satellite zones to export their full file set through a single index.ts barrel (regardless of internal subdirectory layout) would make encapsulation measurable and enforceable — currently partial barrels (data-polling-infrastructure) are more misleading than absent ones because they imply a boundary that does not actually exist for the zone's primary consumer-facing APIs.
- Add a mandatory barrel file policy to zone creation guidelines: any zone with 2 or more consumers in different architectural tiers must provide an index.ts or named barrel that defines its stable public API surface; dashboard-panel-components (2 consumer tiers), web-dashboard (build tooling + UI consumers), and landing-page-assets (build-time consumer) all violate this policy currently
- Add a post-analysis validation step to sourcevision that flags any zone whose ID matches /-\d+$/ (numeric suffix pattern, e.g. web-2, src-2, unit-3) as requiring an explicit pin or merge decision. This mirrors the existing cross-package residual zone dissolution policy but applies it to the intra-package overflow communities that the current policy does not cover.
- Create a web-package zone health tier policy in sourcevision config that applies separate cohesion/coupling thresholds for 'service zones' (threshold: cohesion > 0.75, coupling < 0.25) vs 'composition zones' (threshold: cohesion > 0.50, coupling < 0.50) — the current single global threshold produces false positives for composition-root zones like web-dashboard and false negatives for service zones that approach the lower bound
- The orchestration tier (cli.js, ci.js, web.js) passes CLI command names, flag names, and config key paths as plain strings to child_process.spawn with no schema validation. If a domain package renames a CLI flag or config key, the orchestration script fails silently at runtime with no compile-time or startup-time signal. A lightweight constants file exporting canonical CLI flag names and config key paths, imported by both the domain package and validated in integration tests, would close this drift surface.
- Trigger a fresh sourcevision analysis on the web package and compare the resulting cross-zone import graph against the current zones.json. Multiple high-severity zone insights reference web-viewer → web-server and web-viewer → web-unit violations that appear absent from current source; if confirmed stale, flush and re-enrich to avoid compounding false findings across future enrichment passes.
- 12 zones exceed architectural risk thresholds (cohesion < 0.4, coupling > 0.6): packages-rex:tree-dag-primitives, packages-rex:schema-storage-reporting, packages-rex:task-selection-engine, packages-sourcevision:branch-collection-pipeline, packages-sourcevision:static-analysis-services, packages-rex:cli-mcp-interface, crash-recovery-system, data-polling-infrastructure, packages-rex:prd-structural-validation, packages-rex:prd-proposal-pipeline, packages-rex:integration-adapter-layer, packages-rex:llm-analysis-engine — mandatory refactoring recommended before further development
- Establish an explicit minimum cohesion threshold (e.g. 0.50) as a zone health gate for web-dashboard in sourcevision config; without a hard floor the zone will drift downward with each new mixed-concern file addition and never trigger an automated split recommendation
- Zone "Crash Recovery System" (crash-recovery-system) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.71, cohesion: 0.29, coupling: 0.71) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Add an index.ts barrel file that explicitly lists all public component exports; this gives both consumers (web-application-core and web-dashboard) a single stable import surface and makes it possible to split the zone later without auditing individual import paths across two architectural layers
- Add unit tests for throttle-controls.ts and ws-health-panel.ts — these two components have stateful or event-driven behavior (throttle configuration mutations, WebSocket status display) that warrants the same test coverage as copy-link-button.ts, and their absence creates an untested behavioral surface in the zone's most interactive components
- Move hench-runs.ts to a dedicated views zone or into web-application-core alongside other view-level page components; alternatively rename the zone to 'dashboard-components' to remove the 'panel' qualifier that falsely implies all exports are atomic leaf panels
- Extend polling/index.ts to re-export use-polling.ts and use-polling-suspension.ts alongside the current state/restart utilities; as-is the barrel covers only the polling/ subdirectory while the zone's most-imported file (use-polling.ts, 4 importing zones) is fully exposed without encapsulation.
- Zone "Data Polling Infrastructure" (data-polling-infrastructure) has critical risk (score: 0.67, cohesion: 0.33, coupling: 0.67) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Pin src/shared/glob.ts to the autonomous-agent-engine zone (or a dedicated hench-shared zone). Its current placement in hench-package-config masks a real inter-zone dependency: three autonomous-agent-engine files import it, making autonomous-agent-engine's reported coupling of 0 incorrect and hiding a refactor blast radius.
- Add an E2E smoke test (e.g. verify that index.html references the correct CSS and JS bundle paths after a build) to close the build-correctness gap — the zone's build-time coupling to landing.ts is currently unverified by any automated check
- Add 'use-project-status.test.ts' as a parallel test file to match the two-test-per-two-production-file pattern established by crash-recovery-system — the current single test file mirrors the component filename convention and leaves the hook behaviorally unverified.
- Extract the barrel/re-export declarations from task-usage.ts into a separate index.ts (public API surface only) and keep task-usage.ts as a pure implementation file — this separates the stable re-export contract from the volatile implementation detail and prevents accidental API surface changes during implementation refactors
- Add a comment block or companion manifest enumerating any CDN or external URLs referenced in index.html; without this, supply-chain security tooling cannot audit the HTML shell for outdated or compromised external dependencies invisible to pnpm audit.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @n-dx/web test --reporter=verbose' to confirm boundary-check.test.ts passes without the web-viewer → web-server violation. If it passes, trigger a fresh sourcevision analysis to flush the stale cross-zone metrics — the existing zone insights cite these 4 imports as a critical enforcement gap that may no longer exist.
- cli.js, ci.js, web.js, and config.js are plain JS with no type checking. Adding JSDoc @type annotations tied to package-exported config schemas and enabling tsc --checkJs would surface schema-drift bugs at the orchestration layer without requiring a full TypeScript migration.
- Zone "Tree and DAG Primitives" (packages-rex:tree-dag-primitives) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.94, cohesion: 0.06, coupling: 0.94) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Schema Storage and Reporting" (packages-rex:schema-storage-reporting) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.83, cohesion: 0.17, coupling: 0.83) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Task Selection Engine" (packages-rex:task-selection-engine) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.83, cohesion: 0.17, coupling: 0.83) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Branch Collection Pipeline" (packages-sourcevision:branch-collection-pipeline) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.80, cohesion: 0.20, coupling: 0.80) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "Static Analysis Services" (packages-sourcevision:static-analysis-services) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.79, cohesion: 0.21, coupling: 0.79) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "CLI and MCP Interface" (packages-rex:cli-mcp-interface) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention
- Zone "PRD Structural Validation" (packages-rex:prd-structural-validation) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Zone "PRD Proposal Pipeline" (packages-rex:prd-proposal-pipeline) has critical risk (score: 0.63, cohesion: 0.37, coupling: 0.63) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Zone "Integration Adapter Layer" (packages-rex:integration-adapter-layer) has critical risk (score: 0.63, cohesion: 0.38, coupling: 0.63) — requires refactoring before new feature development
- Zone "LLM Analysis Engine" (packages-rex:llm-analysis-engine) has critical risk (score: 0.62, cohesion: 0.38, coupling: 0.62) — requires refactoring before new feature development
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed 32 suggestion findings: zone pins for hench rex-gateway.ts and glob.ts, components barrel file, CDN manifest comments in HTML files, unit tests for throttle-controls and ws-health-panel, numeric suffix zone validation in sourcevision, and build output smoke tests

items.title: Address pattern issues (3 findings)
items.description:
- crash-detector.ts is placed under performance/ but serves crash recovery, not performance monitoring — the directory name creates a false affordance; relocating to a crash/ subdirectory and adding an index.ts barrel would align location with intent.
- No internal barrel file exists to formalize the hook→component API surface; adding a status/index.ts would allow the hook and component to evolve independently without callers needing to track both module paths.
- No unit or integration tests exist for any of the three page components; given that enrichment-thresholds.ts and suggestions.ts render dynamic analysis output, missing tests create an undetected regression surface for SourceVision output format changes.
items.resolutionDetail: Relocated crash-detector.ts from performance/ to crash/ subdirectory with barrel; added status hook+types to components barrel; created 20 unit tests for 3 sourcevision page components

items.title: Address relationship issues (9 findings)
items.description:
- Without a barrel, crash-detector.ts is directly importable from outside the zone, bypassing any future encapsulation boundary; this is a lower-severity but growing risk as the zone is referenced by 3 external import paths.
- hench-runs.ts is a full view/page that composes panels, yet it lives inside the panel component zone — this inverts the expected composition hierarchy (pages compose panels) and means the zone contains both leaf components and a composite consumer of those components
- web-viewer imports polling 8 times while polling imports web-viewer only 3 times — the dependency is asymmetric and directional (web-viewer depends on polling). The 3 reverse imports from polling into web-viewer should be audited to confirm they are Preact lifecycle calls (useState/useEffect) rather than reaching into unrelated viewer concerns.
- parse-gateway-exports.mjs audits the same gateway rules enforced by tests/e2e/domain-isolation.test.js, but the two are completely decoupled — the E2E test suite never invokes the script, so the script can silently diverge from the enforcement logic without any CI signal.
- Build-time coupling between landing-page-assets (HTML/CSS) and web-dashboard (landing.ts entry point) is opaque to import-graph analysis — zone health metrics show 0 coupling but the zones share a feature boundary; this silent coupling is a maintenance risk if the landing page build pipeline changes
- domain-isolation.test.js enforces gateway rules at the file-import level, but parse-gateway-exports.mjs performs the same analysis outside the test pipeline; if the two analyses ever disagree, there is no automated way to detect the discrepancy — consider driving one from the other.
- Gateway files (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts, external.ts) are embedded inside the largest zone (268 files) rather than in a dedicated integration layer — if web-application-core is later split, these gateways will need to be extracted first to avoid breaking the single-gateway-per-upstream-package invariant
- landing.ts entry point in web-dashboard and the HTML/CSS files in landing-page-assets form a split-ownership pattern for a single feature — neither zone fully owns the landing page, creating a weak zone boundary around this feature
- boundary-check.test.ts enforces the web-internal layering rule (web-server above web-viewer) but the cross-zone import data shows web-viewer → web-server: 4 imports — an upward dependency that this test should be catching; verify whether those 4 imports are type-only (erased at runtime) or genuine runtime violations.
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed all 9 relationship findings: zone pins for gateway files, landing page ownership documentation, boundary-check.test.ts clarifying comment, crash-detector test import migration, gateway-rules.json description update. Several findings were already addressed by prior work (hench-runs.ts already in views/, polling imports confirmed legitimate, parse-gateway-exports.mjs consolidation already done via gateway-rules.json).

items.title: Address anti-pattern issues (16 findings)
items.description:
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) on a 28-directory, 149-file zone is a health-monitoring blind spot: mutual imports across all subsystems lock the cohesion score at 1.0 permanently, preventing automated split recommendations even as semantically unrelated concerns accumulate. Define and enforce a manual decomposition policy (e.g., >100 files OR >15 directories triggers a sub-zone audit) rather than relying on the cohesion metric alone.
- crash-detector.ts is directly importable from any file in the web package with no barrel enforcing single-entry-point access; future callers can bypass use-crash-recovery.ts and consume the detector directly, causing recovery state to diverge from detection state without any type-system or lint guard.
- The zone has two consumers at different architectural depths (web-application-core: 5 imports, web-dashboard: 1 import) but no barrel file defining a stable public API; any internal restructuring propagates to import sites in both zones simultaneously with no contract boundary to absorb the change
- hench-runs.ts is a full page/view that composes panel components, yet it lives in the dashboard-panel-components zone alongside the leaf components it consumes; this creates an intra-zone hierarchy inversion where a page-level file resides in a component-level zone, conflating two different abstraction levels and making it impossible to measure the zone's cohesion accurately without knowing which files are leaves versus composites
- polling-restart.ts has no unit test coverage; restart logic involves stateful transitions that integration and E2E tests are unlikely to exercise exhaustively, leaving a behavioral gap in the polling subsystem.
- use-polling.ts functions as a package-wide hub (4 importing zones) but is assigned to a satellite zone with cohesion 0.33; hub files in satellite zones misrepresent the zone's stability profile and make its coupling metric unreliable — the file's connectivity warrants placement in web-viewer or a dedicated polling-primitives layer.
- parse-gateway-exports.mjs audits gateway exports outside the test pipeline with no automated cross-check against domain-isolation.test.js verdicts; integrate the script into CI and diff its output against test assertions to surface drift at merge time rather than during manual audits.
- domain-isolation.test.js and parse-gateway-exports.mjs enforce overlapping gateway invariants via independent code paths with no shared source of truth; a gateway pattern update that modifies one without the other will pass CI undetected indefinitely.
- use-project-status.ts hook likely has zero dedicated test coverage: the zone's only test file matches the component name, not the hook name, creating an untested stateful module in a zone that already has the minimum viable file count.
- No barrel file exists for the zone, so web-viewer must maintain 3+ direct import paths to individual page module files; any internal rename or split requires coordinated multi-site updates in web-viewer with no single entry point to update.
- Three page components that render sourcevision JSON output have zero test coverage and no schema contract; a breaking change to sourcevision's output format will corrupt all three views silently, with no unit, integration, or snapshot test to detect the regression.
- usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts imports loadPRDSync from prd-io.ts (line 37) directly rather than receiving it through the same dependency-injection channel used for collectAllIds; this undocumented direct coupling partially undermines the DI inversion and means the zone has a second, uncontrolled dependency on web-application-core internals that will not be caught by the existing DI pattern documentation
- web-application-core's 0.92 cohesion metric aggregates both server-layer and viewer-layer files into a single score, hiding the bidirectional imports between these layers (web-viewer→web-server: 4, web-server→web-viewer: 1) that would be flagged as upward-dependency violations under CLAUDE.md's web-internal zone stack if the zone were correctly split
- web-dashboard reaches directly into the polling infrastructure zone (3 imports) bypassing web-application-core as the integration hub; in the stated hub-and-spoke model all cross-zone dependencies from satellite zones should route through web-application-core, making these 3 lateral edges undocumented topology violations
- boundary-check.test.ts flags the web-viewer → web-server import question as an open concern but does not assert against it; the test responsible for enforcing web-internal layering has an unverified gap for the single highest-risk cross-zone edge in the package.
- God function: cmdAnalyze in packages/sourcevision/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions
items.resolutionDetail: Addressed all 16 anti-pattern findings: deleted dead parse-gateway-exports.mjs, added crash-detector runtime exports to performance barrel, added tab-visibility exports to polling barrel, routed cross-zone consumers through barrels, added zone pins for hench-runs.ts and use-polling.ts, wrote unit tests for polling-restart.ts (11 tests) and use-project-status.ts (5 tests), added loadPRD DI seam to usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts, decomposed cmdAnalyze into 4 focused helper functions, verified boundary-check.test.ts already enforces viewer→server boundary

children.title: LLM Proposal Caching for Prune Operations
children.description: Implement caching system for smart prune proposals to enable reuse between dry-run and accept operations without redundant LLM calls

children.title: Implement pending smart prune cache infrastructure
children.description: Create cache file structure, interfaces, and utility functions for smart prune proposals following the reshape.ts pattern with PendingSmartPruneCache interface and file operations
acceptanceCriteria: PENDING_SMART_PRUNE_FILE constant defined as 'pending-smart-prune.json'
PendingSmartPruneCache interface includes generatedAt, prdHash, and proposals fields
savePendingSmartPrune, loadPendingSmartPrune, and clearPendingSmartPrune functions implemented
hashPRD function implemented using SHA256 hash of canonical JSON

children.title: Integrate cache with smart prune workflow
children.description: Wire cache operations into the existing smartPrune function to check cache before LLM calls, save proposals after generation, and clear cache after successful application
acceptanceCriteria: Cache checked before LLM call in smartPrune function with hash validation
Cached proposals used when valid with 'Using cached proposals' log message
Proposals saved to cache after LLM generation with current PRD hash
Cache cleared after successful prune application
Required imports added for crypto and filesystem operations

children.title: Integration tests for smart prune caching workflow
children.description:
Add integration tests to verify the smart prune caching workflow end-to-end. Tests go in packages/rex/tests/integration/cli/commands/prune-cache.test.ts.

The smart prune caching feature (packages/rex/src/cli/commands/prune.ts lines 467-502) caches LLM-generated reshape proposals so that a dry-run followed by --accept reuses proposals without a second LLM call. The cache module is packages/rex/src/core/pending-cache.ts (hashPRD, savePendingSmartPrune, loadPendingSmartPrune, clearPendingSmartPrune).

Unit tests exist in packages/rex/tests/unit/core/pending-cache.test.ts covering the cache module in isolation. These new integration tests must cover the actual smartPrune function's caching behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: cache file (.rex/pending-smart-prune.json) is written after LLM generation with correct prdHash
Test: second smartPrune call with unchanged PRD uses cache (reasonForReshape not called again, 'Using cached proposals' message emitted)
Test: cache is invalidated when PRD changes between runs (hash mismatch triggers fresh LLM call)
Test: cache is cleared after successful apply (accept=true)
All tests mock the LLM layer (vi.mock on reasonForReshape from ../../core/reshape.js) — no real LLM calls
Tests use temp directory with valid .rex/prd.json setup per test case
children.resolutionDetail: Integration tests already exist at packages/rex/tests/integration/cli/commands/prune-cache.test.ts with all 4 acceptance criteria covered and passing.

children.title: PRD Hash Validation for Smart Add Cache
children.description: Add PRD hash validation to smart-add pending proposal cache to prevent applying stale proposals when PRD changes between generation and acceptance

children.title: Add staleness detection for cached proposals
children.description: Implement validation logic in tryAcceptCachedProposals to detect when PRD has changed since cached proposals were generated and handle stale cache appropriately
acceptanceCriteria: Current PRD hash computed and compared with cached prdHash in tryAcceptCachedProposals
Warning message displayed when PRD changes detected: 'PRD has changed since proposals were generated'
Stale cache automatically cleared when hash mismatch found
Backwards compatibility maintained for cache entries without hash
Function returns false when stale cache detected to trigger regeneration

children.title: Implement PRD hash validation in smart-add cache structure
children.description: Update pending proposal cache to include PRD hash metadata and implement hash calculation function for PRD change detection
acceptanceCriteria: hashPRD function implemented using SHA256 of canonical JSON
savePending function updated to accept and store prdHash parameter
loadPending function return type updated to include optional prdHash field
Required crypto and canonical JSON imports added

children.title: Update cache save operations with hash metadata
children.description: Modify all savePending call sites to compute and pass PRD hash, ensuring cache entries include hash metadata for validation
acceptanceCriteria: Both savePending call sites (around lines 1140 and 1326) updated to compute and pass PRD hash
PRD items accessible at call sites for hash computation
Cache structure consistently includes prdHash field across all save operations
Hash computation uses current PRD state at time of caching

children.title: Integration tests for smart-add cache staleness detection
children.description:
Add integration tests to verify the smart-add PRD hash validation and staleness detection. Tests go in packages/rex/tests/integration/cli/commands/smart-add-cache.test.ts.

The smart-add caching feature (packages/rex/src/cli/commands/smart-add.ts) saves a prdHash alongside cached proposals via savePending(). When --accept is used, replayCachedIfRequested() (line ~1129) loads cached proposals, computes current PRD hash, and compares. If hashes differ, it warns and clears stale cache.

The prdHash is passed through maybeCacheSmartAddProposals() at three call sites (lines ~1376, ~1496, ~1532) using hashPRD(existing) where existing is the current PRD items array.

Key functions to test:
- savePending (line 706): should include prdHash in JSON
- loadPending (line 714): should return prdHash from JSON  
- replayCachedIfRequested (line ~1127): staleness check logic
- maybeCacheSmartAddProposals (line ~1303): hash passthrough
acceptanceCriteria: Test: savePending writes prdHash to pending-proposals.json when provided
Test: loadPending returns prdHash from cached file
Test: --accept (replayCachedIfRequested) succeeds when PRD hash matches cached hash
Test: --accept rejects stale cache when PRD has changed (warns and clears cache, returns false)
Test: --accept works with backward-compatible cache files that have no prdHash field (proceeds without validation)
Test: maybeCacheSmartAddProposals passes hash through to savePending
All tests mock the LLM layer — no real LLM calls
Tests use temp directory with valid .rex/ setup per test case
children.resolutionDetail: All 6 integration tests already exist in packages/rex/tests/integration/cli/commands/smart-add-cache.test.ts and pass. Tests cover: savePending prdHash writing, loadPending prdHash reading, --accept with matching hash, --accept stale cache rejection, backward-compatible cache without prdHash, and maybeCacheSmartAddProposals hash passthrough. All use mocked LLM and temp directories.

items.title: Address observation issues (16 findings)
items.description:
- 1 circular dependency chain detected — see imports.json for details
- Bidirectional coupling: "prd-fix-command" ↔ "rex-prd-management-core" (7+1 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
- Bidirectional coupling: "task-usage-analytics-gateway" ↔ "web-application-core" (1+33 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects
- High coupling (0.75) — 7 imports target "rex-prd-management-core"
- Low cohesion (0.25) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- Coupling of 0.75 combined with cohesion of 0.25 indicates this zone is more of a thin adapter over rex-unit than a cohesive domain — the core fix logic in src/core/fix.ts should likely be absorbed into rex-prd-management-core to eliminate the reverse import dependency.
- The bidirectional import cycle (fix depends on rex-unit for 7 symbols; rex-unit imports 1 symbol from fix) is an architectural inversion — CLI-layer modules should never be imported by the domain core; the shared symbol should be moved to a neutral location within rex-unit.
- The bidirectional import relationship with the fix zone (rex-unit imports from fix: 1 import) is worth auditing — a core domain module importing from a CLI command zone suggests a possible layering inversion where fix logic should live in core rather than in the CLI command file.
- High coupling (0.6) — 5 imports target "web-application-core"
- Cohesion of 0.4 is at the warning threshold; the inclusion of token-usage-nav.test.ts (likely testing a component in web-viewer) slightly dilutes the zone's focus — consider whether that test belongs here or closer to its component.
- Coupling of 0.6 is at the warning threshold due to 5 outbound imports into web-viewer; verify these are one-directional type imports (e.g. for shared state shapes) rather than runtime component dependencies that invert the viewer layering.
- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- High coupling (0.6) — 14 imports target "web-application-core"
- Bidirectional cross-zone imports with web-viewer (14 each direction) indicate a circular dependency between this zone and the core application zone; one direction should be eliminated.
- Cohesion of 0.4 and coupling of 0.6 are both at threshold; the zone combines static assets, build config, and viewer components with no shared import backbone — consider splitting package config from viewer code.
items.resolutionDetail: Fixed 3 concrete architectural issues: (1) eliminated rex core/move.ts → cli/errors.ts layering violation by replacing CLIError with plain Error, (2) broke circular dependency chain polling→polling-restart→graceful-degradation→memory-monitor→polling by importing registerPollingSource directly from polling-state.ts instead of the barrel, (3) removed cross-zone coupling in usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts → prd-io.ts by making loadPRD an injected dependency. Remaining findings are informational zone-metric observations (cohesion/coupling scores, entry point counts, fan-in hotspots) that reflect expected architectural patterns.

items.title: Address suggestion issues (36 findings)
items.description:
- Create a packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts (alongside rex-gateway.ts) to concentrate all @n-dx/llm-client imports into a single file. This mirrors the existing rex-gateway.ts pattern and completes the gateway discipline for both upstream dependencies hench has. Without it, llm-client imports are unconstrained and may drift across the 159-file zone as agent capabilities expand.
- Add an explicit assertion to architecture-policy.test.js that enforces zero inbound imports into any file in packages/rex/src/ and packages/sourcevision/src/ from the @n-dx/web package. The current domain-isolation.test.js guards gateway imports but does not exhaustively guard against web-to-domain import direction inversions. The two confirmed violations (sourcevision→web-server, rex-unit→web-server) passed CI, indicating the current test does not cover these paths.
- Both domain packages lack a tests/integration/ directory tier. Establish a shared convention (e.g., a TESTING.md in the monorepo root) that mandates an integration test tier for each package and specifies what in-process contract scenarios it must cover — particularly gateway re-exports and store mutation correctness — before the integration gap widens further.
- CLAUDE.md prohibits backwards-compatibility re-export shims, but packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts is exactly such a shim — created when crash-detector moved to crash/ and never cleaned up. Audit all re-export-only files across the codebase for the same pattern: any file whose entire content is re-exports pointing to a new canonical location is a shim candidate for deletion after updating its importers.
- Create packages/web/tests/unit/shared/ and relocate tests for web-shared utilities (node-culler.test.ts) from tests/unit/viewer/ into it. Enforce the convention in boundary-check.test.ts: a test file whose primary production import target is classified in zone X must reside under a directory corresponding to X, not a sibling zone's directory.
- Establish a documented admission criterion for the cross-package integration tier: any new gateway module (rex-gateway, domain-gateway, external.ts) must have a corresponding integration test added to tests/integration/contracts/ within the same PR that introduces the gateway. This prevents the current situation where 8-function gateways exist with zero integration-tier coverage.
- Introduce a cohesion CI gate at threshold 0.5: the current bimodal distribution (all healthy zones ≥0.95, all problem zones ≤0.44) means this threshold would catch all current structural outliers (fix, web, route, web-shared) without flagging any currently healthy zone, making it a zero-false-positive enforcement rule.
- The '"./dist/*": "./dist/*"' wildcard subpath export present in all package.json files (as documented in CLAUDE.md's conventions table) creates a package-wide bypass of the public.ts API contract. Adding a lint rule or CI check that flags direct dist/ imports in consumer files (e.g., import from 'rex/dist/internal/foo') would convert this undocumented escape hatch from an implicit footgun into an explicit, tracked exception.
- The CLAUDE.md gateway table entry for hench lists '8 functions (store, tree, task selection)' — verify hench/src/prd/rex-gateway.ts export count matches this number. If it has grown beyond 8 without a documentation update, the 'explicit, auditable surface' guarantee for the gateway pattern is broken and the table should be updated to reflect the current count.
- The cross-zone import map shows 'rex-unit → web-server' and 'sourcevision → web-server' as the two domain-tier violations, but neither 'rex-unit' nor 'sourcevision' matches the canonical zone IDs 'rex-prd-management-core' and 'sourcevision-analysis-engine' — zone alias resolution in the import map may be silently masking which actual zone owns the violating file, making targeted remediation unreliable. Audit the alias mapping to confirm which files are responsible.
- The e2e test file cli-dev.test.js is a single point of failure for dev-mode coverage analogous to scheduler-startup.test.js for server boot. Both files should be annotated in CI configuration as required tests (not skippable) to prevent silent coverage gaps when refactoring dev-server startup paths.
- The intra-domain isolation rule ('rex and sourcevision never import each other') is stated in CLAUDE.md but has no corresponding test assertion. Add a single test in domain-isolation.test.js that scans packages/rex/src/ for any import matching 'sourcevision' and packages/sourcevision/src/ for any import matching 'rex|@n-dx/rex'. This closes the one remaining enforcement gap in the four-tier hierarchy: horizontal domain-tier isolation is currently trust-based rather than test-enforced.
- The three single-edge cycle-completing imports (web-server → web-viewer, web-viewer → route, web-viewer → web-unit) have persisted across multiple analysis passes with no remediation. Create discrete tracked tasks for each edge with the specific file and symbol to relocate — open-ended observations about bidirectional cycles do not drive resolution; named tasks with specific targets do.
- The viewer-message-pipeline zone (src/viewer/messaging/, 7 files) is prescribed as an independent architectural tier in CLAUDE.md but has no corresponding detected zone and no independent health metrics. Add archetype annotations to messaging files so zone tooling can surface their coupling independently from the broader web-application-core zone.
- Add subdirectory-level barrel files (analyze/index.ts, store/index.ts, schema/index.ts) that explicitly whitelist the symbols each sub-directory exposes internally; this converts the currently convention-based intra-zone layering into a mechanically enforceable boundary that would have prevented the reverse import from the fix zone.
- Split the sourcevision test tiers into a separate 'sourcevision-tests' zone analogous to rex-cli-e2e-tests — bundling all production source and all test tiers into one 144-file zone prevents per-tier cohesion metrics from being computed and hides test coverage distribution from zone-level health tooling.
- Cohesion of 0.96 across 355 files is statistically insensitive: any individual file change moves the metric by ≤0.003. Do not rely on cohesion thresholds in CI to detect early structural regressions in this zone — supplement with direct import-graph edge count assertions on the specific boundary files (external.ts, rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) instead.
- The viewer-message-pipeline internal layer (src/viewer/messaging/, 7 files) is subsumed into this zone with no independent health metrics. Assigning archetype annotations to the messaging files would allow zone tooling to surface its coupling independently without requiring a full re-analysis.
- Zone name 'peripheral' contradicts quantitative coupling (0.6): a zone with 14 bidirectional imports to core is not peripheral by any structural measure. Rename to reflect its actual role (e.g., 'viewer-supplemental-components') to avoid misguiding contributors who use the name as a priority signal during triage.
- Audit crash zone's 3 imports from web-viewer to determine if the internal cohesion (1.0) is achieved by coupling all four files to the same web-viewer type or hook. If so, the zone's high cohesion score is misleading — it reflects shared viewer-layer coupling rather than independent crash-recovery design. The goal should be cohesion around crash semantics, not cohesion around a shared viewer dependency.
- Zone name 'crash-recovery-system' implies ownership of both crash detection and recovery, but the production recovery hook (use-crash-recovery.ts) lives in packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ — outside this zone. The zone actually owns only a re-export shim and tests. Rename the zone to reflect its actual scope, or consolidate the canonical crash/crash-detector.ts and use-crash-recovery.ts into a single dedicated crash/ zone separate from web-viewer.
- packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts is a re-export shim pointing to ../crash/crash-detector.ts and violates CLAUDE.md's explicit prohibition on backwards-compat re-export shims. Identify all importers of the performance/ path and update them to import from src/viewer/crash/ directly, then delete the shim. The shim is also the reason the zone analysis misidentifies this zone's canonical production file.
- Add an integration test for the hench→rex-gateway pipeline: instantiate a PRDStore, have the gateway select a task, and verify status update propagates correctly. This is the highest-value missing contract test given hench's rex-gateway.ts imports 8 functions from rex.
- The fix zone has cohesion 0.25 AND bidirectional coupling with rex-unit — both the fragile-zone risk indicators simultaneously. This combination (low cohesion + bidirectional coupling) means changes to either zone require understanding both, with no stable interface between them. This is the highest-risk intra-domain structural combination in the codebase and warrants immediate remediation over web-package cycles, which are better documented.
- The 2-file integration tier should grow proportionally with the number of cross-zone import edges, not the number of packages. With 15 cross-zone edges currently tracked, a minimum of 5-7 integration test files (one per architectural boundary: web-server↔web-viewer, viewer↔web-shared, hench↔rex-gateway, web↔domain, etc.) would provide boundary-specific failure messages rather than a single omnibus failure.
- The './dist/*' wildcard subpath export in package.json creates a bypass path around public.ts for all packages including llm-client. For the foundation tier, this is particularly risky: hench or a future consumer could import llm-client internals directly, then have those imports break silently when internal files are renamed. Consider restricting the llm-client package.json to only the '.' export pointing to public.ts and removing the dist wildcard, or documenting the wildcard as an intentional escape hatch with a stability disclaimer.
- Add a test in tests/e2e/architecture-policy.test.js that parses each root orchestration script (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) for static import/require statements and fails if any are found. The spawn-only boundary is the most important architectural rule in the codebase but is currently enforced only by convention — making it machine-checkable would catch accidental violations before they reach main.
- Update integration-coverage-policy.test.js to enforce a minimum ratio (e.g., integration tests >= 15% of e2e test count) rather than a static floor, so the policy stays proportional as the suite grows.
- Add a cli-mcp.test.ts to this zone that validates the 'rex mcp' command startup and basic tool invocation through the stdio transport; the current nine test files cover all CLI commands except MCP, leaving an untested entry point in the rex command surface.
- Create a dedicated tests/integration/ directory within the rex package with at least one test covering the store→tree→task-selection pipeline in-process; the current direct jump from unit to E2E means store mutation regressions require a full CLI spawn to detect.
- A 4-file zone with coupling 0.6 means the majority of its import edges are external — this zone has more cross-zone connections than internal ones. Zones with coupling > cohesion are structural liabilities: they import more than they encapsulate. Either absorb route into web-shared (for primitives) or web-viewer (for components) based on which host better reflects each file's actual purpose.
- register-scheduler.ts and shared-types.ts lack unit test counterparts despite the zone establishing an implicit 1:1 pairing convention. Add tests/unit/server/register-scheduler.test.ts and tests/unit/server/shared-types.test.ts (or a shared types validation test) to restore consistent coverage.
- viewer-route-state is the only zone with both cohesion (0.4) and coupling (0.6) simultaneously at warning thresholds — the fragile zone anti-pattern. As a merger candidate: move route-state.ts (pure routing logic) into web-shared and use-route-state.ts (Preact hook) into web-viewer proper. This eliminates the zone, resolves both metrics, and removes the bidirectional cycle with web-viewer in a single redistribution.
- The 'web' zone (28 files, cohesion 0.40) participates in a confirmed 14+14 bidirectional cycle with web-viewer, has low internal cohesion, and carries a misleading name. This combination of three independent risk indicators (low cohesion, confirmed cycle, naming collision) makes it the highest-priority zone rename and structural audit target outside the direct-violation fixes.
- Introduce an explicit admission criterion for web-shared: 'a file belongs in web-shared only if it (1) has zero framework imports (no Preact, no Express), (2) is consumed by at least two distinct layers above it, and (3) exposes a cohesive abstraction'. Without this criterion, web-shared functions as a residual zone — the place files go when they don't fit anywhere else — which explains both its low cohesion and its role as a target for cycle-breaking relocations.
- Create a src/viewer/api.ts (or src/viewer/index.ts) barrel that explicitly re-exports the symbols consumed by crash, route, web-unit, and web zones. This single file would serve as the inbound API contract for web-viewer, making its external-facing surface visible, auditable, and decoupled from internal module organization. The existence of external.ts for outbound imports but no equivalent for inbound imports is an asymmetry in the current gateway discipline.

5. Verify/update hench gateway export count in CLAUDE.md

7. Move node-culler.test.ts to tests/unit/shared/
children.resolutionDetail: Created llm-gateway.ts (30 functions + 10 types), deleted crash-detector shim, moved node-culler test, added gateway pipeline integration tests, created TESTING.md, updated CLAUDE.md gateway table, added proportional ratio enforcement

6. Annotate cli-dev.test.js and scheduler-startup.test.js as required tests

Also fixed pre-existing zone cycle detection bug (packageFamily was based on colon-splitting zone IDs instead of deriving from file paths).

4. Add register-scheduler.test.ts and shared-types.test.ts
